info.description:
This is Humand Public API's Documentation. To use this api you will have to send an api key in the Authorization HTTP header as follows: 'Basic {{api_key}}'

## Rate Limiting

The API implements rate limiting to ensure fair usage and protect the platform. If you exceed the rate limit, you will receive a `429 Too Many Requests` response.

All responses include the following headers to help you monitor your API usage:

- **RateLimit-Policy**: Describes the rate limit policy applied. Format: `{limit};w={window_in_seconds}`

  Example: `50;w=60` (50 requests per 60-second window)

- **RateLimit**: Shows your current rate limit status. Format: `limit={max}, remaining={remaining}, reset={seconds_until_reset}`

  Example: `limit=50, remaining=45, reset=30`

- **Retry-After**: (only included in 429 responses) Number of seconds to wait before retrying

post.summary: Manual correction or overwrite of time off balances
post.description:
Allows manual correction of time off balances using three operations:
- ADDITION: Adds amount to current balance (incremental)
- SUBTRACTION: Subtracts amount from current balance (incremental)
- SET: Sets balance to specified amount (overwrites current balance)

For SET operation:
- BASIC_ANNUAL policies: Sets available balance
- UNLIMITED policies: Sets days used

The accreditationYear parameter is optional. If not provided, uses current cycle.

put.description: Update the state of a Time Off Request. Supports rejecting, approving, or cancelling requests. When approving, all pending approval steps are automatically approved. For cancellation, only approved requests can be cancelled and the balance will be reverted.

approved.description: Approve a time-off request, automatically approving all pending steps

cancelled.description: Cancel an approved time-off request. The balance of the approved request will be reverted.

put.description: Update the start and end dates of a Time Off Request. Optionally, you can also update the consumption types (FULL_DAY or HALF_DAY) for the start and end dates. If consumption types are not provided, the existing values will be preserved. The request must not be fully approved. Follows the same validation rules as creating a new request.

updateDatesOnly.description: Update only the start and end dates of a time-off request, keeping existing consumption types

updateDatesWithConsumption.description: Update dates and set specific consumption types for both start and end dates

changeToHalfDays.description: Update dates and change both dates to half-day consumption

changeOnlyEndDate.description: Update dates and change only the end date consumption type

get.description:
**DEPRECATED:** This endpoint is deprecated. Please use the new Marty API instead: https://api-prod.humand.co/marty/docs/swagger-ui/index.html#/

Obtain all chats where the receiver is involved. The receiver must be a bot and only 1-1 chats are returned.

**DEPRECATED:** This endpoint is deprecated. Please use the new Marty API instead: https://api-prod.humand.co/marty/docs/swagger-ui/index.html#/

Get messages sent to a receiver, unread flag filters unread messages, receiverEmployeeInternalId filters messages sent to a specific receiver. The receiver must be a bot. This endpoint does not change the unread status of the messages returned.

post.description:
Generates a detailed report of all course tasks for a specific user and sends it via email.
The report includes:
- User information (ID, activation date, job positions)
- Segmentation groups and their values
- All course tasks with their status, dates, duration, and grades
- Learning path associations

200.description: Report generation initiated successfully. The report will be sent to the provided email.

Unread.name: unread
Unread.description: To filter unread chats or messages (depending on endpoint). If false, return all chats/messages

ReceiverEmployeeInternalId.name: receiverEmployeeInternalId
ReceiverEmployeeInternalId.description: A receiver is used for chats and messages. A receiver for chats lets you filter all chats where the receiver belongs to, meanwhile a receiver for messages lets you filter all messages sent to the receiver.

Public.name: public
Public.description: Whether if the file must be public or not

Order.name: order
Order.description: Used to select the sort type for response columns (asc o desc)

CreationDate.name: creationDate
CreationDate.description: Used to get results by creation date

TimeOffRequestStates.name: states
TimeOffRequestStates.description: Used to get results by states

PolicyTypeIds.name: policyTypeIds
PolicyTypeIds.description: Used to get results by policy type ids

PolicyIds.name: policyIds
PolicyIds.description: Used to get results by policy ids

TimeOffFromDate.name: fromDate
TimeOffFromDate.description: Retrieve results based on a starting date. Includes all requests with days from this date onwards.

TimeOffToDate.name: toDate
TimeOffToDate.description: Retrieve results based on and end date. Includes all requests up to and including this date.

TimeOffResolutionFromDate.name: resolutionFromDate
TimeOffResolutionFromDate.description: Retrieve results for which the review process ended after this date. Includes all requests with resolution date from this date onwards.

TimeOffResolutionToDate.name: resolutionToDate
TimeOffResolutionToDate.description: Retrieve results for which the review process before after this date. Includes all requests with resolution date up to and including this date.

TimeOffCreatedAtSince.name: createdAtSince
TimeOffCreatedAtSince.description: Retrieve results created on or after this date. Includes all requests with creation date from this date onwards.

TimeTrackingEntryStartDate.name: startDate
TimeTrackingEntryStartDate.description: Start date (inclusive) to filter time tracking entries. Only entries with a timestamp on or after this date will be retrieved.

TimeTrackingEntryEndDate.name: endDate
TimeTrackingEntryEndDate.description: End date (inclusive) to filter time tracking entries. Only entries with a timestamp up to and including this date will be retrieved.

password.description: Only used for creating users, not when updating one.

operation.description:
- ADDITION: Adds amount to current balance
- SUBTRACTION: Subtracts amount from current balance
- SET: Sets balance to the specified amount (overwrites current balance)

amount.description:
For ADDITION/SUBTRACTION: amount to add/subtract (must be positive).
For SET: target balance value (can be positive, negative, or zero).

observations.description: Optional observations for the balance correction

accreditationYear.description:
Optional. Year of the cycle to apply the correction.
If not specified, uses the current cycle.

fromDate.description: Start date of the time-off request

toDate.description: End date of the time-off request

fromConsumptionType.description: Optional. Consumption type for the start date (HALF_DAY or FULL_DAY). If not provided, keeps the existing value.

toConsumptionType.description: Optional. Consumption type for the end date (HALF_DAY or FULL_DAY). If not provided, keeps the existing value.

signatureCoordinates.description: Array of PDF coordinates where signatures should be placed. Each coordinate specifies the position and dimensions of a signature field on a specific page.
Array of PDF coordinates where signatures should be placed. Each coordinate specifies the position and dimensions of a signature field on a specific page.

creationUrl.description: The URL used to create the signed resource.

amountInTime.description: Days requested in time (not sold for money). Calculated as amountRequested - amountInMoney.

amountInMoney.description: Days sold for money. Null or 0 if balance sale is not used.

documentation.description: Array of URLs for attached documents. Empty or omitted if no documents are attached.

now.description: The time for creating the entry. Can be past but not future

TimeTrackingDaySummaryHours.description: Consolidated hour counters for the day

employees.description: List of employees and their shift assignments. Maximum 100 employees per request.

days.description: List of days with shift assignments. Maximum 30 days per employee.

timeSlots.description: List of time slots for the day. Each time slot is an array with start and end times in HH:mm format.

isWorkday.description: Indicates if this is a working day

skipped.description: Shift assignments that were skipped with the reason

isWorkday.description: Indicates if this is a working day

timeSlots.description: List of time slots for the day

employeeInternalId.description: The employee internal ID of the user to generate the report for

Rate limit reached, please try again later

RateLimit-Policy.description: Describes the rate limit policy applied. Format: {limit};w={window_in_seconds}. Example: 50;w=60 means 50 requests per 60 second window

RateLimit.description: Current rate limit status. Format: limit={max}, remaining={remaining}, reset={seconds_until_reset}. Example: limit=50, remaining=0, reset=42

Retry-After.description: Number of seconds to wait before making another request
